def: s.m. Friday.
s.f. A kind of jasmine, Jasminum auriculatum; a sort of perfume; small cardamoms.

adj. (f. -ā), Subtle, minute, atomic, atom-like; little, small; slender, thin, fine, attenuated; delicate, tender; exquisite, refined; nice; sharp, acute; shrill; ingenious; artful, crafty; accurate, correct, exact, precise; — s.m. An atom; fine thread; subtlety; craft, fraud; an epithet of the Supreme Spirit; — the clearing-nut plant
subs: s.f. Sharp wit, acute intellect, mental acumen; — adj. Sharp-witted, witty, acute, shrewd, intelligent, penetrating, ingenious; abstracted
and sūkshma-darśī, adj. & s.m. Sharp-sighted, keen-eyed; of acute discernment; acute, quick, intelligent; — a sharp-sighted person, an acute person
s.m. (in Philos.) 'The atom-like body,' the subtle body which is invested by, and is the archetype or pattern of, the gross material frame.

def: adj. incorrect for sūkshma, q.v.
s.f. = S سوکشمتو सूक्ष्मत्व sūkshmatva, s.m. Thinness, fineness; minuteness, subtlety; acuteness; shrillness.
s.m. = S سوکشمتا सूक्षमता sūkshmatā, s.f. Thinness, fineness; minuteness, subtlety; acuteness; shrillness.
s.m. corr. of sūkt, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Dry, dried up, lean, thin, emaciated.
more: (from سوکهنا sūkhnā, q. v. or शुष्क ) adj. Lean, thin, dry, emaciated. ¶ sikṭā ( शर्करा ) s. m. Potsherd, shard.

def: adj. = sukṭā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Dry, dried up, lean, thin, emaciated.
s.m. What is well spoken; a sentence; an invocation, a Vedic metrical prayer; a hymn

ety: the preceding, with इका for कः
def: s.f. Dried fish; — a withered old woman.
more: (see سکٿا sukṭā ) 1. adj. Lean, thin, emaciated, dry. 2. s. f. Dried fish.

def: s.f. Oyster; mussel, cockle; snail.

s.f. A pearl-oyster; an oyster-shell; a small shell; a muscle, a cockle; a conch-shell; a portion of the skull (used as a cup, &c.); a curl or feather on a horse's neck or breast; a sort of perfume; a disease of the cornea; a weight of two karshas or four tolās
subs: s.m. Name of one of the seven principal mountains or mountain ranges of India.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit शक्ति (śakti)

def: adj. & s.m. Of good family, well-born; — a good or virtuous family; — name of a caste of Brāhmaṇs.
adj. (f. -ā or -ī) & s.m. Beautifully young or youthful; very soft or tender; smooth; delicate; elegant; — a beautiful youth.
more: adj. Soft, delicate. See سکوار sukwār.

def: s.f. Softness, tenderness, smoothness, delicateness; youthfulness; elegance.

ety: inf. n. of سکن 'to be still,' &c.
def: s.m. Quiet, quietude, tranquillity, rest, peace; cessation; a mute.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سُکُون (sukūn), from Arabic سُكُون (sukūn).

ety: for A. سکونة, fr. sukūn, q.v.
def: s.f. Dwelling, residence, habitation; — rest, tranquillity, contentment ( = sukūn).
more: n. f. 1. Habitation ( bās ). 2. Illit . Contentment. sukūnat-pizīr h. , v. n. To reside.
(from سکن) s. f. 1. Residence, dwelling, habitation. 2. Tranquillity.

def: s.m. corr. of 1˚ śūkar; 2˚ sukar, qq.v. adj. & s.m. Doing good, beneficent, benevolent; — easy, practicable, feasible; — doing good, well-doing, beneficence, charity, benevolence.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شکر (šakar, “sugar”).

ety: inf. n. of سکت 'to be silent'
def: s.f. Silence, quietness, peace.
more: n. f. سکت was silent. Silence; taciturnity.
(from سکت) s. f. Silence, quietness, peace. ¶ sakūt, adj. Silent, quiet.

def: s.m. A kind of vetch.
more: n. m. A kind of vetch.
s. m. A kind of vetch.

def: adj. Soft, tender; delicate, feeble.
s.m. Friday (see s.v. suk). s.m. (f. -ī), A parrot.

s.m. (f. p), A sharp or acute pain; colic; stomach-ache; indigestion; gout; rheumatism; — any sharp or pointed weapon; a pike, dart, spear, lance, spike; a thorn, prickle; the point of a spear, &c.; — sympathy, compassion, tenderness.
more: n. m. S. शूल 1. ( sūal ) A thorn; a prick. 2. The point of a spear, pike, or dark. 3. Pain; ache; grief. Khar siyār bolahīṅ pritikūlā, Suni suni hoë Bharat ur sūlā . Rāmāyan. 4. Colic. 5. Clearing the land. sūl sī lagnā , v. n. To prick; pierce. Tumhārī bāt sūl sī lagtī hai .
s. m. f. 1. ( शूल ) Colick. 2. A trident, pike, dart. 3. The point of a spear. 4. ( शूक ) A thorn. 5. Compassion, tenderness.

def: s.m. Condition, position, situation.

s.m. A sharp or acute pain; pain in the stomach, colic; rheumatism; gout; — any sharp or pointed weapon, a pike, dart, spear, lance; a trident; a stake; a spit; an iron pin; — a banner, ensign; death, dying; the ninth astronomical yoga
subs: adj. & s.m. Holding a trident in the hand; — an epithet of Śiva
s.m. Name of a division of the infernal regions
s.m. lit. 'Enemy of colic,' the castor-oil plant, Ricinus communis
s.m. 'Destroyer of pain,' an epithet of Śiva
s.m. or śūl-hat, s.f. 'Removing colic'; assafœtida.

def: s.f. A spit; a stake (for impaling criminals; — see sūlī); — a harlot; — a brick-kiln.
more: s. m. Ӕschynomene paludosa: Roxb. (The wood of which, being very light and spongy, is used by fishermen for floating their nets. A variety of toys, such as artificial birds and flowers, are made of it. Garlands of those flowers are used in marriage ceremonies. When charred it answers the purpose of tinder).

def: adj. = su-labh, q.v.
adj. Easy to be obtained, easy of acquisition; easy to be perceived or effected; easy of access; attainable, feasible, easy
subs: (˚bha+av˚), adj. Easily gaining room or admission.

def: adj. Easy ( āsān ). Ritu Rasant bahe tribidh beyārī. Sab kahīṅ sulabh padārath chārī .
s.f. Easiness of acquisition or attainment, feasibleness.
adj. = su-labh, q.v.

s.m. = su-lakshaṇ, q.v. s.m. A good or distinct mark; an auspicious mark or sign or omen; good conduct; excellence; beauty; a determining; — adj. Clearly distinguished by marks; of good omen, auspicious, favourable, promising, fortunate; well-behaved; clever, expert; beautiful; excellent.
more: n. m. S. सुलक्षण 1. An auspicious sign or omen. 2. Good conduct ( bhalāchalan ).
s. m. Good distinguishing mark or character; adj. Clearly or strongly distinguished by (marks, features, or) character. ( Carey ).

def: adj. 1. Auspicious; promising; favorable. 2. Well-behaved. 3. ( Ironic .) Ill-behaved; wicked; bad. Āo putr su-lachchhane, ghar hū ko le jāo! Prov. Come, my fine boy! bring ruin on the house!

ety: sulag˚ prob. = S. संलग(ति), rt. लग् with सम्
def: v.n. To be kindled, be ignited, be set light to; to light; to burn without smoke or flame, to burn clearly or brightly; to be inflamed, be excited, be roused, be irritated.
more: v. n. See sulgānā , v. a. 1. E lauṅṛyā, laṛikan meṅ haṭakī dao, jin khelan jāë, hameṅ kāhe sulgautī hau? Rus. Farrukh. Girl, I've told you not to play with boys. Why do you set me on fire?
v. n. 1. To light, to be kindled, to be inflamed. 2. To burn without smoke or flame.

def: s.m. pl. (of ṣāliḥ, q.v.), The just, the virtuous, the pious; men of wisdom or counsel.
more: s. plur. (of صالح) Pious, upright men.

def: prop. n. Solomon (the son of David).
more: (Heb. ) n. prop. Solomon the son of David.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سلیمان (soleymân), from Arabic سُلَيْمَان (sulaymān).

def: adj. Of or belonging to Solomon; — s.f. The state or dignity of Solomon; — s.m. ( = sulaimānī patthar), The onyx.
more: 1. adj. Of or belonging to Solomon. 2. s. m. An onyx. 3. s. f. The state of Solomon.

def: v.n. To be or become unravelled, to be disentangled; to be solved; to be analysed, tested or tried; to be settled, be set right again
more: v. n. See suljhānā , v. a. Ādam ke vaqt kā sūt uljhā paṛā hai, ab kyā sul- jhegā? (Said of religious controversy).
v. n. To be unravelled or disentangled.
subs: part. (of sulajhnā and suljhānā), Ready, disentangled, &c.; tested, proved, experienced.

def: adj. (dakh.) See سلکهن sulakkh̤aṇ.

adj. = su-lakshaṇ, q.v. s.m. A good or distinct mark; an auspicious mark or sign or omen; good conduct; excellence; beauty; a determining; — adj. Clearly distinguished by marks; of good omen, auspicious, favourable, promising, fortunate; well-behaved; clever, expert; beautiful; excellent.

s.m. A good or distinct mark; an auspicious mark or sign or omen; good conduct; excellence; beauty; a determining; — adj. Clearly distinguished by marks; of good omen, auspicious, favourable, promising, fortunate; well-behaved; clever, expert; beautiful; excellent.
more: (i. q. سلچهن sulachchh̤an, q. v. in dakh.) adj. 1. Clever: see چتر chatur. 2. Of good marks or features; beautiful.

def: v. n. (dakh.) See سلگنا sulagnā.

s.m. A good or distinct mark; an auspicious mark or sign or omen; good conduct; excellence; beauty; a determining; — adj. Clearly distinguished by marks; of good omen, auspicious, favourable, promising, fortunate; well-behaved; clever, expert; beautiful; excellent.
adj. = su-lakshaṇ.
v.t. To cause to sleep, to put to sleep; to hush, lull, quiet, calm, allay; to lay, repress, quell, subdue; to kill, murder
more: v. a. Caus. of سونا q. v. 1. To put to sleep; to rock or sing a child to sleep. 2. To quiet; allay; calm; lull; still; hush. 3. To lay; repress; smother; quell; subdue. 4. ( sulā denā ) To give a quietus to; to kill. sulāne-vālī , adj. ( Med .) Soporific.
( शायन or causal of سونا) v. a. 1. To cause or put to sleep, to repose. 2. To kill, to murder. سلا دينا sulā denā, To kill, to murder.

def: adv. Three and three together, three at a time, by threes.
adj. 'Of, or relating to, three things'; comprising or composed of three (radical) letters (a word), triliteral; made up, or consisting of, three parts, triple; three-sided, triangular.
more: adj. 1. ( Gram .) Triliteral. 2. ( Math .) Three-sided; triangular.
adj. 1. Triangular, any thing made up of three parts, triple. 2. (in gram.) Triliteral.

def: v.t. To turn round again, to restore to a right position, to set right again; to arrange or settle (a matter or affair, with another); — v.n. To come or turn round again to a right position, to get or come right again
v. a. To manage an affair; settle or settle with. Tum koï na bolo, maiṅ akelā sulaṭ lūṅgā!
s.m. 'The backbone'; the loins; the back (esp. the lumbar portion); (met.) offspring.
adj. Of or relating to the loins, lumbar; proceeding from the loins of, descended or sprung from; legitimate.
more: adj. 1. Descended or sprung from. 2. Legitimate.

def: s.m. A small ball of tobacco smoked in a ḥuqqa without the intervention of a tile
more: s. m. Little balls of tobacco smoked in a ḥuḳḳa without the intervention of a tile.
subs: v.t. To burn to ashes; to consume
v.n. To be consumed; to be utterly destroyed or ruined.

def: n. m. Little balls of tobacco smoked in a huqqā , without the intervention of a tile; (Slang) charas , q. v. Pāṅch ṭake kī botal, sulfā ek ṭake kā pīnā, Roz tujhe samjhātī, bhaṛve, gujrā ek mahīnā . Play. Ten coppers for a bottle of wine, two coppers for a pipe, A whole month I have counselled you, still you will smoke and swipe. sulfā k. , v. a. 1. To burn to ashes. 2. To consume; spent. sulfā h. , Slang. v. n. To be consumed, spent. Unkā sab māl sulfā ho gayā, ek kauṛī nahīṅ rahī .
v.t. To light, kindle, ignite, to cause to burn brightly; to inflame, incite, stir up, rouse.
more: v. a. S. संलग्न 1. See jalānā , and uksānā , (7.) 2. To ignite; strike a light; stir a fire. 3. To incite; rouse; stir up; irritate.
(causal of سلگنا sulagnā ) v. a. To light, to inflame, to kindle.

ety: fr. muṣālaḥat, 'making peace,' inf. n. iii of صلح; see ṣalāh
def: s.f. Peace, reconciliation, truce, agreement, concord; compromise; treaty
more: s. f. Peace, reconciliation, treaty, truce, concord. صلح کل ṣulḥ-i-kull, s. f. Perfect reconciliation, definitive treaty.
subs: s.f. A voluntary reconciliation; a voluntary compromise
s.f. The act of breaking the peace; a breach of the peace
adj. & s.m. Peaceful; peace-making, amicable, conciliatory; — a peaceful or conciliatory person; peace-maker
s.f. The state of being amicable or peaceable, amicableness, conciliatoriness, &c.
s.f. Perfect reconciliation; permanent or durable peace
(-se), To make peace (with), to be reconciled (to); to make a truce, come to terms (with), &c.
or kar-denā (-meṅ), or sulḥ karwānā (-meṅ), To make peace (between), to reconcile
s.f. Perfect reconciliation; definitive treaty; — adj. & s.m. Desirous of conciliating all, peaceful, friends with all; — one who is desirous of conciliating all, a peaceful man
s.m. A written deed of pacification; a treaty of peace; a deed of compromise
v.n. Peace to be concluded; reconciliation to take place.

def: s.m. Friendship.
adv. Peaceably, pacifically, amicably.
s.f. An impaling stake; — gallows, gibbet; — (fig.) acute suffering, torture, agony; risk of life; hard labour
more: n. f. S. शूल 1. A stake (for impaling criminals); a gibbet; gallows. Chaṛhā Mansūr sūlī par pukārā ishqbāzon ko, 'Yeh uske bām kā zīnah hai, āë jiskā jī chāhe!' Mansūr impaled himself, but first all lovers counselled he, 'Behold the steps the top to reach, come on who will with me!' 2. Risk of life; danger. 3. Hard labour. 1. Sūlī par kī rotī khātī hai . Prov. The bread I eat is accompanied by the tortures of impalement. (She suffers acutely at the hands of her supporter). 2. Āṭh pahar sūlī hai . Wom. Every hour of the day and night I sufter the tortures of impalement. sūlī pe jān honā , v. n. 1. To be in constant hazard of one's life. 2. To suffer continued torture. sūlī pe chaṛhānā, sūlī chaṛhānā yā denā , v. a. 1. To impale (a criminal). 2. ( Wom .) To give pain ( satānā , 2.). Khāvand kī bad-mizājī ne mujhe sūlī par chaṛhā rakkhā hai . Wom. sūlī dene-vālā , n. m. 1. One who impales. …
1. s. f. ( शूला ) An empaling stake. 2. s. m. ( शूली ) A name of Mahādeva. سولي دينا sūlī denā, or سولي چڙهانا sūlī chaṛhānā, To empale.
subs: To suffer torture or agony (like that of impalement); to be in constant fear of (one's) life
or sūlī ćaṛhānā, or sūlī denā (-ko), To impale; to hang; to crucify; to torture, give pain (to)
s.m. One who impales; — a hangman, executioner.

def: adj. Suffering sharp pain; having the colic; — s.m. An epithet of Śiva (as holding a trident).

adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Suffering sharp pain; afflicted with colic; suffering from rheumatism; — armed with a spear or pike, carrying a lance or stake &c.; — one who suffers sharp pain, &c.; — a spearman, pikeman, lancer; an epithet of Mahādeʼo or Śiva.
s.f. See سولي sūlī.
corr. of śūlī and sulī, qq.v. s.f. An impaling stake; — gallows, gibbet; — (fig.) acute suffering, torture, agony; risk of life; hard labour
subs: To suffer torture or agony (like that of impalement); to be in constant fear of (one's) life
or sūlī ćaṛhānā, or sūlī denā (-ko), To impale; to hang; to crucify; to torture, give pain (to)
s.m. One who impales; — a hangman, executioner.

def: v.t. To unravel, to disentangle; to solve (a difficulty or a problem); to analyse, to examine, test, prove; to set right or straight again, to settle, arrange.
more: v. a. Opp. of uljhānā . S. शोधन 1. To unravel; disentangle. 2. To settle a case ( faislah k .). suljhā suljhāyā , adj. Tried; experienced. sujhāv'ā, suljhāo , 1. Disentanglement. 2. Solution (of a problem) 3. Settlement.
(causal of سلجهنا) v. a. To unravel, to disentangle.

r: suljhāʼo, suljhāwā, suljhā, nā, āʼo, āwā
def: s.m. Unravelling, disentanglement; solution; settlement.

r: suljhāwā, suljhāʼo, suljhā, nā, āʼo, āwā
def: s.m. Unravelling, disentanglement; solution; settlement.
s.m. A ladder; staircase.
s.m. A third, a third part or portion; — a kind of Arabic hand-writing.
more: n. m. A third part; one-third.
s. m. 1. The third part. 2. An Arabick hand-writing.

def: s.f. = sursurī, q.v. Creeping, crawling; rustling; — creeping sensation, titillation, irritation (produced by the crawling of ants, &c. over the body), formication; tingling, pricking (of a limb or member asleep); — hissing, whizzing; a firework, a squib; — an insect bred in grain, weevil.

ety: v.n. fr. سلط 'to overcome'; 'to possess dominion,' &c.
def: s.m. Sultan, emperor, king, prince, sovereign, ruler.
more: n. m. A sultan; sovereign. sultānah , n. f. An empress.
(from سلط Heb. To have power, to rule) s. m. A prince, a sovereign, king, emperor, sultan.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سلطان (sultān), from Arabic سُلْطَان (sulṭān).

ety: for A. سلطانة sult̤ānat, fem. of sultān
def: s.f. Empress; queen.
adj. Of or belonging or relating to a sovereign, royal, regal, princely; — s.f. Dignity or office of sult̤ān; sovereignty, empire, dominion ( = salt̤anat); — a kind of broadcloth.
more: adj. Relating to the sovereign; royal.
1. adj. Regal, princely, belonging to a sovereign. 2. s. f. A kind of broad cloth. 3. Sovereignty, dominion.

def: corr. of sult̤ānī, q.v.

s.m. Journey, road, way; institution, rule, mode, manner; behaviour or conduct (to or towards), treatment, usage; civility, attention, kindness; intercourse, amicable intercourse or footing, good terms or understanding (with); cessation of hostilities, peace
more: n. m. سلک string. 1. Behaviour towards one; treatment. 2. Peace (ايکا 1). sulūk se rahnā , v. n. To live on good terms. sulūk k. , v. a. 1. To treat; behave to. Chalte hāth pāoṅ sulūk kar lo . Prov. Saying. Do good while you may. 2. ( sulūk se pesh ānā ) To behave well to; to oblige. 3. To be reconciled, at peace. sulūk karvānā , v. a. To make peace; reconcile.
(from سلک) s. m. 1. Road, way. 2. Manner, mode, conduct. 3. Intercourse, usage, treatment, civility, kindness, attention. سلوک کرنا sulūk karnā, To treat, proceed with, treat with kindness, befriend.
subs: adv. With civility or kindness, kindly; properly, well; in amicable intercourse, on good terms, peaceably (e.g. sulūk-se pesh ānā, To treat or receive kindly; to behave properly or well; — sulūk-se rahnā, To live on good terms)
(-se), To proceed (with); to act or behave (towards), to treat; to treat kindly, to befriend, to behave well (to); to be reconciled (to), make peace (with).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سُلُوک (sulūk), from Arabic سُلُوك (sulūk). Compare Punjabi سَلُوک (salūk) / ਸਲੂਕ (salūk) and Gujarati સલૂક (salūk).

def: v.t. To cause to be put to sleep, to have (one) put to sleep, &c. (see sulānā and sonā).

ety: Pehl. śumb, śum; Zend śafa; S. शफ
def: s.m. A hoof, hoofed foot
more: n. m. A hoof (of a horse, etc.). Aisī laṛāī huī ki ghoṛoṅ ke sum khūn meṅ ḍūb gaë. sum lenā , 1. (v. a.) To pare the hoof. 2. (v. n.) To trip, stumble (a horse).
subs: To pare the hoofs (of, -ke); — to trip, stumble (a horse).

def: s.m. A flower.

ety: prob. corr. of A. شوم shūm
def: s.m. A miser, niggard.
more: n. m. sūmṛī , n. f. Cor. of A. شوم A miser; a niggard. 1. Mār sūm kī gor par tīn lāt! Beggars. Three kicks on the miser's grave! 2. Ques. Jorū pūchhe sūm se 'kāhe badan malīn, Ke kuchh khoyo gāṅṭh ko, ke kāu kū dīn?' Ans. 'Nā kachhu khoyo gānṭh ko, nā kāū kū dīn Deto dekho aur ko, jā se badan malīn .' The wife her miser husband asks 'what makes you, dear, so low? What have you lost, or is it alms perchance you did bestow?' 'I've nothing lost, nor have I aught in charity bestowed, But alms seen by another given my soul with grief hath bowed.' sūm-pan, sūm-panā , n. m. Niggardliness.
s. m. 1. The moon. 2. Monday. سوموار सोमवार som-wār, s. m. Monday.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سوم (siwum).

ety: v.n. fr. سمق 'to be tall or high,' 'to overtop'
def: adj. Pure sheer, unmixed
more: (prop. summāḳ ) s. m. 1. Sumach (Rhus coriaria). 2. Porphyry, the hardest species of marble.
subs: s.m. A vessel for rice (it consists of two boiling pots, one fitting into the other, the inner pot being perforated full of small holes: when the rice is boiled the inner vessel is lifted out, leaving the water strained off in the exterior pot).
wety: From Arabic سُمَّاق (summāq).

def: adj. Well-garlanded, possessed of a good garland.

adj. & s.m. Very charming, fair, beautiful, handsome; of good disposition, good-minded, well-disposed, benevolent; well-pleased, satisfied, happy; — a learned man, a teacher; a student of the Vedas; wheat; the thorn-apple, Datura metel; a flower; the great-flowered jasmine
more: 1. s. m. A flower (in general). 2. adj. Good hearted, benevolent, generous.
subs: and suman-dhanush, s.m. Epithet of the bow of Kāmdeva

def: s.m. An epithet of lake Mānas.
s.f. The great-flowered jasmine ( = su-man).
s. Name of an ancient idol temple in Gujarāt: also, the name of the idol.
adj. Very fortunate or auspicious, bringing good fortune; abounding in sacrifices.
adj. Very captivating, very charming, very agreeable or pleasing, delightful, beautiful.
adj. & s.m. Well deliberated, well-advised; — good advice ( = next, q.v.).
s.m. = S سمنترنا सुमन्त्रणा su-mantraṇā, s.f. Goodadvice, wise counsel.
s.f. = S سمنترن सुमन्त्रण su-mantraṇ, s.m. Goodadvice, wise counsel.
adj. Dying easily.
1˚ adj. corr. of su-mar, q.v.; — 2˚ s.m. corr. of shumār, q.v.

ety: Prk. सुमरणअं; S. स्मरणीयं, rt. स्मृ
def: v.t. To remember, recollect, call to mind, keep in memory; to invoke, to call on (God); to mention.
more: v. n. 1. To remember; keep in memory. 1. Koī 'Rām Rām' kah-kar sumre, koī bole 'Shiv Shiv', 'Harī Harī,' Kyā dānā, daiyat, dev aṭal, kyā rāchhas, devat, jinn o parī! Nazīr. Some 'Ram Ram' call, some cry 'Shiv Shiv,' and some cry 'Harī Harī.' Or demon, giant, monster, devil, god, Jin or a fairy. 2. Harī sumaran kīnī nahīṅ, kaise sukh sarsāë? 2. more: adj. S. शून्य 1. Empty; void; uninhabited; desert; desolate. 1. Sūnī sār se markhanā bail achchhā . Prov. Better a vicious ox than an empty stall. 2. Sau gulāmoṅ ghar sūnā . Prov. Although a hundred slaves there be, no master there the house appears empty. 3. Sonā lene pī gaë, sūnā kar gaë des, Sonā milā na pī phire, rūpā ho gaë kes . My husband went in search of gold, the country round in stillness mourned, He found no gold, he came not back, my hair to silvery white was turned. 4. Sūnā ghar bhiṛoṅ kā rāj . Prov. The empty house is the wasps' estate. 2. Still; silent; quiet; calm; peaceful. Ekānte, nadī tīre, athwā sūn mandare! 'Rām rām' japo, bhāī, apnī mokshī kārne! Alone by river side, or in secluded fane, Brother! 'Ram Ram' repeat and your salvation gain. 3. Unprotected; unguarded. Sūnī khetī chhoḍ-kar hānḍe hān kuhān, Oh to bind ku-bind saë, nāhīṅ jāt Kisānṛ . Rus. The field unheed…

My love went to earn gold, and left the country desolate; I could not sleep (or he found not gold) my love returned not, my hair became silver.

more: v. a. S. श्रू 1. Caus. of sunnā , q. v. 1. Vā ghar kī mohe koī na sunāve jā ghar se jīyā āyā ho, Man kā mahram un des kā ek na milā, divānā ho! Kabīr. Aj kī rain ghaṭat hai chandarvā, chāṅd ke kāran tū kyoṅ gahe ri! Song. Jhanjhauṭī . My pretty one! dont in the moonlight sleep! who else should you advise? Tonight the moon will be eclipsed, why seized should be the lovelier prize? (Rāhu would leave the moon and seize you who are more fair and beautiful). 3. Tohrā le sundar balamuā, seho to gailan par-des ho! E. Wom. song. Jhūmar . My husband's handsomer than you, away in foreign parts! 2. Good; effective. Muṅh se na bolat the so khusāmad karat haiṅ, Main ne kaiso sundar jādū chalāë dīno rī! Saiyāṅ ne mo ko garvā lagāë līno rī! Wom. song. I would not speak, so he to coax me then began, Say aint I skilled in use of powerful talisman? To clasp me to his breast my husband then began.

def: s.m. & f. A box, trunk, chest, coffer, case; a coffin.
more: n. m. Dim. sanduqcha, sanduqchī . 1. A box; coffer; chest; case. 2. Coffin; trunk. Umdoṅ ke tan ko tāṅbe ke sandūq meṅ dharā, Muflis kā tan paṛā rahā, māṭī upar paṛā, Qāim na yāṅ pe yeh, aur na sābit wo vāṅ rahā, Dono ko khāk khā gaī, āge kahūṅ maiṅ kyā! Jo khāk se banā haī wo ākhir ko khāk hai! Nazīr. The bodies of the great in copper coffins lay, The corpses of the poor not covered even with clay, Nor this as 'twas remains, nor that entire doth stay, Both dust to dust return, what more can man aught say? Of dust 'twas made, to dust it crumbles all away! sandūq'ī , adj. 1. Like a box (a tomb, scabbard). 2. ( Arch .) Oblong (a pillar).
s. f. and m. A box, a trunk, cabinet, case, chest, coffer, coffin. (Plur. صناديق).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَنْدُوق (sandūq), borrowed from Arabic صَنْدُوق (ṣandūq), borrowed from Aramaic צנדוק (ṣəndūq, “coffer, especially one storing documents, safe-guarding precious items”), borrowed from Ancient Greek σῠνθήκη (sŭnthḗkē), from σῠντῐ́θημῐ (sŭntĭ́thēmĭ, “to place or put together”), from συν- (sun-) + τίθημι (títhēmi).

def: v.t. To smell, sniff, snuff, inhale, scent, nose
more: v. a. To smell; inhale. 1. Ek nāṛ bhauṅrā sī kālī, kān nahīṅ aur pahre bālī, Nāk ṅahīṅ aur sūṅghe phūl, jetā araj tetā tūl . Riddle. Ans. Ḍhāl . Shield. A female blacker than the bee no ears yet earrings wears, No nose yet flowers smells, as broad as long in front appears. 2. Sūṅgh-ke pahannā! A jest. First smell and then put on (your shoe)!

v. n. S. श्रवण, Pr. sunanam , Pālī, savanam , 1. To hear; hearken; listen. 1. Sunte sunte kān bahre ho gaë . Prov. Saying. I am deaf with all this dinning in my ears. 2. Būṛhe kalāṅvat kī kaun sune? Prov. Who would listen to an old man's song? 3. Santan kī bānī sune prem sahit jo koë, Gangā-dik sab tīrth-phal bin asnānai hoë . Who lovingly the saint's words in his breast doth constant keep, Without ablutions shall all Shrines' and Ganges' blessings reap. 4. Maiṅ ne apne kānoṅ se sunā hai . I heard it with my own ears. 2. To hear abuse; to be abused. 1. Abhī kuchh sunoge! 2. Mār gālī sunte haiṅ, gumāshte kahlāte haiṅ! Prov. Abuse and kicks he's treated to, a manager so called, parblieu! 3. Ek kaho na das suno . Prov. Nor say one word nor hear ten. 3. To hear or entertain (a case). Muqadma sunnā . 4. To attend to; regard; mind; observe. sunā an-sunā k. , v. a. To pretend not to hear. Sone …
1. ( श्रवण ) v. a. To hear, to hearken, listen or attend to; s. m. Hearing. 2. ( शून्य ) s. m. A cypher.

ety: for A. سنة, v.n. fr. سن 'to form or fashion,' 'to institute,' &c.
def: s.f. Mode or manner of acting, course or rule of conduct or of life, right way (of life); statute, ordinance (of religion), rite, an obligatory religious ceremony; circumcision; a practice or saying (or the practices and sayings collectively) of Mohammad as handed down by tradition, the traditions of Mohammad; — the prayers enjoined by Mohammad
more: n. f. سن instituted. lit . a religious ceremony. 1. Any obligatory religious ceremony, as circumcision ( khatnah ). 2. The traditional law based upon the sayings and doings of Mahommad, scarcely inferior in authority to the text of the Quran in the estimation of the Sunnīs , although not recognised by the Shīās. Mard ko chār nikāh sunnat hain . 3. The prayers enjoined by Mohammad. Farz to paṛh līye, sunnateṅ bāqī haiṅ. sunnat k. , v. a. To circumcise. sunnat-i-rakat , Ceremonial observances enjoined upon the authority of tradition, distinguished as mavakkad absolute, and gair-mavakkad not absolute. be-sunnat , adj. Uncircumcised.

s. f. ( lit. a religious ceremony necessary to be performed; from سنّ To be constantly observing, to follow rule, or from Heb. To repeat, q. d. that which is constantly to be repeated, observed or performed) 1. Circumcision. سنت کرنا sunnat karnā, v. a. To circumcise. 2. The traditions of Muhammad, ordinance, institute, rite, right way (of life). ¶ sanat (Heb. q. d. the repetition of the same course of the sun, with its concomitant changes) s. f. A year.
s. f. An institute or ceremony mentioned in the traditions of Muhammad, and not of divine origin.

more: n. f. 1. Betel-nut; the fruit of the areca catechu chhālyā . 2. ( supārah ) Glans penis. Taṅbolan kī laṛkī se kīje na yārī, Dikhā-kar sarautā katar le supārī . Double entendre. Of tobacconist's daughter beware! if you to her will stick. Her clipping tool just shewn she 'll cut your nut off slick.
s. f. 1. Betel-nut. (Areca catechu). 2. Glans penis.

adj. Of good end.
s.m. Security, preservation, safety, happiness.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سپاس (sipās).

def: adj. Well-kept, preserved, secure, safe, happy.
s.m. A good road; good conduct; orthodoxy.
adj. Walking in the right path, well-behaved; orthodox.
s.m. A good or suitable vessel; an earthen vessel; — a fit or competent person, an able man; a worthy person, good man, respectable man; — adj. Fit, suitable; good, worthy, respectable.
more: adj. lit . a good vessel. Worthy; fit; creditable. supātr ; Sant. bhāge hoṛ , n. m. A good man; a worthy person.

def: s.m. The leaf of the Laurus cassia.
s.f. = supaidī or sufaidī, q.v.
adj. Bearing much or good fruit, very fertile, very fruitful; very profitable; fruitful for good, attended with good results, advantageous, useful, beneficial, efficacious.
more: adj. S. सुफल, Pr. sabhalam . Fruitful; productive; useful ( phaldār, phaldāyak ). 1. Suphal pungī phul, kadlī, rasālā, Rope bakul, kadamb, tamālā . Rāmāyan. 2. Bhayeū manorath suphal tab sun, Girrāj kumār, Pariharu dusah kalesh sab ab miliheṅ trapurār . Rāmāyan. 3. Hā hā! badan ughār drig suphal karo sub koë! Roj sarojan ke paṛe haṅsī sasī kī hoë . Dohā. Unveil that lovely face sweet maid, and glad the eyes of all around! Nay, nay! the lily's bloom will fade, and taunts the vanquished moon confound.
adj. Bearing good fruit (literally or figuratively), fruitful, profitable, useful.
wety: From Sanskrit सफल (saphala).
